name: Suvan Sharma
title: Aspiring Computer Science Engineer | AI & Game Development Enthusiast
tagline: Syosset High School student — Class of 2027 — passionate about building impactful technology, combining AI, software development, and creativity to solve real-world problems.
intro: Welcome to my site. Explore my projects, interests, and an AI assistant that answers from my profile.
bio:
I am a highly motivated high school student at Syosset High School with a strong passion for computer science, artificial intelligence, robotics, and game development. I enjoy building innovative solutions that combine technical skills with creativity.

Through experiences like the MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute and developing my own mobile applications, I have explored how technology can be used to solve real-world problems, especially in areas like mental health and education.

Beyond academics, I actively contribute to my community through leadership and volunteer work, mentoring younger students in coding and organizing large-scale service initiatives. I strive to grow not only as a developer but also as a leader and problem solver.

projects.title: AI Serious Game (MIT BWSI)
projects.description: Collaborated on a team to design and develop a game integrating AI-driven decision making. Applied machine learning concepts and algorithmic thinking, with a focus on real-world simulation and intelligent gameplay systems.

projects.title: Advanced STEM Certifications — MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute
projects.description: Completed advanced coursework in AI fundamentals and applied machine learning, with hands-on training in algorithmic problem solving and simulation systems.

projects.title: NextStep App
projects.description: A Flutter/Dart mobile app focused on teen mental health support and resources. Designed UI/UX, navigation, and backend logic, with features to guide users toward helpful resources and solutions. Code: NextStep-Life-Unblocked

projects.title: Divergent Team Hackathon — 2nd Place, State Winner
projects.description: Divergent Hackathons 2025–26 are youth-driven, project-based events that empower high school students to take on real-world challenges in STEM, creativity, and leadership. Hosted at Microsoft facilities across 15 cities, these hackathons unite students, mentors, and community leaders through internships, coaching, and a youth-led execution model. With 100 students per city and a National Championship in Washington, D.C. (June 2026), Divergent Hackathons inspire innovation, teamwork, and community impact on a national scale.

projects.title: Science Research Projects
projects.description: Conducted experiments on planarian regeneration and E. coli antimicrobial resistance. Built a linear regression model in Python for data analysis and prediction.

projects.title: STEM Coach — Long Island Tutoring Academy
projects.description: Taught coding and STEM concepts to 20+ students. Delivered 100+ hours of instruction using Scratch and robotics. Helped students build interactive games and strengthen problem-solving skills.

projects.title: Junior Firefighter Training Program
projects.description: Trained in emergency response and safety procedures. Developed teamwork, discipline, and leadership under pressure.

hobbies.title: Game design & AI exploration
hobbies.description: I enjoy designing and prototyping original video games, focusing on gameplay mechanics and player decision-making. Recently I have been exploring how artificial intelligence can enhance game behavior and create more dynamic, adaptive experiences.

hobbies.title: Independent coding & building
hobbies.description: Outside of coursework, I regularly build personal projects to deepen my understanding of programming. I enjoy experimenting with technologies like Flutter and Python, and I am especially interested in applications that solve real-world problems.

hobbies.title: Robotics & intelligent systems
hobbies.description: Interested in intelligent systems that combine programming, sensors, and automation. I like exploring how robotics can address real needs in healthcare, education, and accessibility through AI-driven and logic-based systems.

hobbies.title: Classical piano
hobbies.description: As a NYSSMA Level 6 pianist, I have trained in classical music for several years. Piano has helped me develop discipline, attention to detail, and creative expression, which I apply to my academic and technical work.

hobbies.title: Athletics & endurance training
hobbies.description: Through varsity track, competitive swimming, and tennis, I have built resilience, time management, and a strong work ethic. I enjoy pushing my limits and improving performance.

hobbies.title: Mentorship & community engagement
hobbies.description: I am passionate about teaching and mentoring younger students in coding and STEM. I enjoy breaking down complex ideas and helping others build confidence through hands-on learning.

hobbies.title: Continuous learning & curiosity
hobbies.description: I pursue learning beyond the classroom—new programming ideas, language skills, emerging technologies like AI, and hands-on programs such as Junior Firefighter Training for teamwork, discipline, emergency-response fundamentals, and community safety awareness.

passionProjects.title: Community service & leadership (TLCA & Sri Sathya Sai)
passionProjects.description:
Active contributor through the Telugu Literary and Cultural Association (TLCA) and the Sri Sathya Sai International Organization: food and clothing drives, book drives, hygiene kits and donations, and cultural and wellness outreach.

Congressional Award (Gold) — recognized for leadership and service through these initiatives.

passionProjects.title: Youth mentorship & education (Long Island Tutoring Academy)
passionProjects.description:
Mentored younger students in academics and foundational STEM through structured tutoring, focusing on confidence and skill building.

Presidential Volunteer Service Award (Gold) — recognized for sustained educational mentorship and impact.

passionProjects.title: Special education & assistive technology (Shyog School)
passionProjects.description:
Volunteered with Shyog School for special-needs children, supporting inclusive learning and students who need additional developmental support.

Currently developing an early-detection app focused on ADHD and brain development, aimed at earlier identification and better developmental outcomes.

aiAlterEgo.name: Digital You
aiAlterEgo.description: Ask questions about my background, projects, and interests. Answers are based on the information on this site.
